vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. The desk environment shows a severe lack of biological material.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: Yellow pot is present but appears empty/devoid of foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No evidence of Mexican Mint.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: Pothos and White Rabbit anchor are absent/not visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No evidence of Silver Guest.

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; soil surface appears undisturbed and barren.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot appears empty; no green matter detected.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot appears empty; no white rabbit anchor detected.
plant_audit.P4: Black pot appears empty; no plant matter detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Uniformly dark, suggesting high moisture or shadow-induced low contrast; no visible cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: A metallic object (spoon) appears in T-2, suggesting human intervention/maintenance, but it is removed in subsequent frames.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-3: Initial state shows minimal visibility; T-3 reveals the yellow pot and black pots more clearly.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Introduction of a metallic spoon into the yellow pot area; potential maintenance or soil disturbance.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: Removal of the spoon; return to a static, empty state across all pots.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: Total systemic failure. There is no visible plant life in any of the registered containers. The biome is currently non-functional.
anomalies: Metallic spoon (T-2) - likely a tool for soil aeration or substrate adjustment.
Absence of the White Rabbit scale anchor in all frames.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete absence of the expected botanical specimens. The environment appears to be a set of empty containers. The presence of a spoon in the T-2 frame suggests an attempt at intervention, but the current state remains devoid of any living plant tissue. The lack of the scale anchor (White Rabbit) indicates a significant change in the desk's physical configuration compared to the registry.
